channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-13854] lustre-MDD0000: next log does not exist!
item.description:
Trying to create a file after setting up changelogs on a fresh new FS yields an IO error:

# lustre/tests/llmount.sh
(...)
# lctl set_param mdd.*.changelog_mask=MTIME
mdd.lustre-MDT0000.changelog_mask=MTIME
# lctl --device lustre-MDT0000 changelog_register
lustre-MDT0000: Registered changelog userid 'cl1'
#touch /mnt/lustre/file
touch: setting times of '/mnt/lustre/file': Input/output error

dmesg provides some interesting information:

# dmesg -H | tail -n 3
[Aug 5 11:23] Lustre: lustre-MDD0000: changelog on
[Aug 5 11:25] LustreError: 23303:0:(llog_cat.c:544:llog_cat_current_log()) lustre-MDD0000: next log does not exist!
[  +0.003231] LustreError: 24631:0:(llite_lib.c:1707:ll_md_setattr()) md_setattr fails: rc = -5

Happy hunting
item.summary: lustre-MDD0000: next log does not exist!

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Hiya,

we hit this bug on a new filesystem with 2.15.8 servers and 2.15.7 clients.
it happened as soon as we started making user directories - ie. a loop over

{ mkdir, chown, chmod }

.

any chance this patch can be backported to 2.15?

for now we're running with our own port of it. seems to work.

cheers,
robin

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58681/
Subject: LU-13854 mdd: setattr can use multiple chglog types
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 5ca924c14a02bd86daf9a3bdd5b90671116fcf83
hmm, I had to push it tomorrow...
elliswilson thanks for the super detailed and helpful report!

"Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58681
Subject: LU-13854 mdd: setattr can use multiple chglog recs
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 6a6b86fd0374235a82628146d6500d27abed74e1

I have continued to see this pop up across all versions of 2.14 and 2.15, and most recently repro'd it against master (bb6a2d2e80f04645b488ecca6ba14cb628e3eeb3).  Here is a quick way to reproduce it:

On the MDT, register changelogs and give a mask that will pick up attribute changes:

lctl --device lustrefs-MDT0000 changelog_register
lctl set_param -P "mdd.${CLUSTER_FILESYSTEM_NAME}-MDT${MDT_IDX_HEX}.changelog_mask"=all-ATIME-FLRW-GXATR-MARK-MIGRT-NOPEN-OPEN-RESYNC-XATTR-LYOUT

On a client, confirm that the changelog is functional by touching a file and getting the changelogs.

Create around million files quickly (I don't think you need this many, this only hits reliably after a chown/chmod storm has been running for a bit).  Feel free to create them however you like.  I just did:
cd /lustre/
/usr/laaso/bin/mpiexec -map-by node -np 10 /usr/laaso/bin/mdtest -C -F -b 4 -w 1024 -I 74 -z 5

Get a list of all of our files, and then run a bunch of processes performing chowns (you may need to switch to root here – just make sure you're chowning to a new user than what the original mdtest used):
find . -type f > ~/files
cat ~/files | xargs -n 30 -P 10 chown elliswilson:

The above will run successfully for about 10 seconds on my client, and then will begin to spew things of the form:
chown: changing ownership of './out/test-dir.0-0/mdtest_tree.0/mdtest_tree.2/mdtest_tree.11/mdtest_tree.46/mdtest_tree.188/mdtest_tree.754/file.mdtest.2.55840': Input/output error
chown: changing ownership of './out/test-dir.0-0/mdtest_tree.0/mdtest_tree.2/mdtest_tree.11/mdtest_tree.46/mdtest_tree.188/mdtest_tree.754/file.mdtest.8.55826': Input/output error
chown: changing ownership of './out/test-dir.0-0/mdtest_tree.0/mdtest_tree.2/mdtest_tree.11/mdtest_tree.46/mdtest_tree.188/mdtest_tree.754/file.mdtest.3.55865': Input/output error
chown: changing ownership of './out/test-dir.0-0/mdtest_tree.0/mdtest_tree.2/mdtest_tree.11/mdtest_tree.46/mdtest_tree.188/mdtest_tree.754/file.mdtest.5.55839': Input/output error
chown: changing ownership of './out/test-dir.0-0/mdtest_tree.0/mdtest_tree.2/mdtest_tree.11/mdtest_tree.46/mdtest_tree.188/mdtest_tree.754/file.mdtest.9.55806': Input/output error
chown: changing ownership of './out/test-dir.0-0/mdtest_tree.0/mdtest_tree.2/mdtest_tree.11/mdtest_tree.46/mdtest_tree.188/mdtest_tree.754/file.mdtest.5.55796': Input/output error
chown: changing ownership of './out/test-dir.0-0/mdtest_tree.0/mdtest_tree.2/mdtest_tree.11/mdtest_tree.46/mdtest_tree.188/mdtest_tree.754/file.mdtest.6.55860': Input/output error

On the client I see:
[ 3699.338933] LustreError: 13794:0:(llite_lib.c:1934:ll_md_setattr()) md_setattr fails: rc = -5
[ 3699.344080] LustreError: 13794:0:(llite_lib.c:1934:ll_md_setattr()) Skipped 55 previous similar messages
[ 3699.847766] LustreError: 13992:0:(llite_lib.c:1934:ll_md_setattr()) md_setattr fails: rc = -5
[ 3699.852878] LustreError: 13992:0:(llite_lib.c:1934:ll_md_setattr()) Skipped 5868 previous similar messages
[ 3700.846884] LustreError: 14393:0:(llite_lib.c:1934:ll_md_setattr()) md_setattr fails: rc = -5
[ 3700.851832] LustreError: 14393:0:(llite_lib.c:1934:ll_md_setattr()) Skipped 11885 previous similar messages

On the MDT I see:
[ 4135.649693] LustreError: 11287:0:(llog_cat.c:542:llog_cat_current_log()) lustrefs-MDD0000: next log does not exist!
[ 4136.151546] LustreError: 11286:0:(llog_cat.c:542:llog_cat_current_log()) lustrefs-MDD0000: next log does not exist!
[ 4136.158563] LustreError: 11286:0:(llog_cat.c:542:llog_cat_current_log()) Skipped 5927 previous similar messages
[ 4137.151386] LustreError: 11279:0:(llog_cat.c:542:llog_cat_current_log()) lustrefs-MDD0000: next log does not exist!
[ 4137.157477] LustreError: 11279:0:(llog_cat.c:542:llog_cat_current_log()) Skipped 11971 previous similar messages

This will NOT reproduce if you remove CTIME and SATTR from the changelog mask, but then you won't get any recorded changelogs for chowns, so it's totally unclear to me if the IOEs are somehow related to incorrect locking to insert changelogs surrounding those two types of changelog entries, or if this is just a race/buffering problem that materializes at very high ingestion rates.

Our Lustre servers are on Ubuntu 20.04.6, so it's entirely possible that there is something specific about that and the ldiskfs patch series that causes this to materialize for our changelogs but it's not hitting a bunch of others.  Or perhaps nobody else both has sufficiently high attribute changing workloads and changelogs enabled with the necessary masks (I doubt it).  If somebody has a lustre cluster and 20 minutes to see if they can repro this I'd be really appreciative.

I believe I ran into this:

root@node:/# chmod -R 777 /lustrefs
chmod: changing permissions of '/lustrefs': Input/output error
root@node:/# dmesg -T
...
[Mon Jun 27 17:25:18 2022] LustreError: 1259:0:(llite_lib.c:1712:ll_md_setattr()) md_setattr fails: rc = -5

Looking at the original error, I see the following at the exact time of chmod failure on the MDT:
[250875.733525] LustreError: 32002:0:(llog_cat.c:544:llog_cat_current_log()) lustrefs-MDD0000: next log does not exist!
 
There are no surrounding lines that suggest there weren't free slots or something else to indicate why this is happening.
 
The description seems to suggest the changelog mask may be involved.  On my clusters I use:
mdd.lustrefs-MDT0000.changelog_mask=all-ATIME-FLRW-GXATR-MARK-MIGRT-NOPEN-OPEN-RESYNC-XATTR
 
Looking across the many clusters this bug is hit approximately once per day.  Are there any suggestions for ways I could help provide additional debugging information?  Also, is there a reason to not return EAGAIN here rather than EIO (I'm not intimately familiar with this code so I'm sure there is a good reason)?  Looking at this block it feels like just trying again would be reasonable (and certainly these nodes do recover – just that single command gets EIO back).  Failing a whole job for just a one-off retryable concurrency issue is unfortunate.
 
   536   /* Sigh, the chd_next_log and chd_current_log is initialized   537    * in declare phase, and we do not serialize the catlog
   538    * accessing, so it might be possible the llog creation
   539    * thread (see llog_cat_declare_add_rec()) did not create
   540    * llog successfully, then the following thread might
   541    * meet this situation. */
   542   if (IS_ERR_OR_NULL(cathandle->u.chd.chd_next_log)) {
   543     CERROR("%s: next log does not exist!\n",
   544            cathandle->lgh_ctxt->loc_obd->obd_name);
   545     loghandle = ERR_PTR(-EIO);
   546     if (cathandle->u.chd.chd_next_log == NULL) {
   547       /* Store the error in chd_next_log, so
   548        * the following process can get correct
   549        * failure value */
   550       cathandle->u.chd.chd_next_log = loghandle;
{{   551     }}}
   552     GOTO(out_unlock, loghandle);

No, this was just a test system I setup to review a patch.
This is not something we hit in production (yet).
Is this something that you are planning to investigate?
